name: terraform-workspace
description: AI-powered Terraform workspace manager backed by GitHub Actions and HappyPathway infrastructure patterns

chatParticipants.name: terraform
chatParticipants.description: AI-powered Terraform workspace manager. Generate code, run plans/applies via GitHub Actions, manage secrets and environments.

commands.name: generate
commands.description: Generate new Terraform HCL from a description

commands.name: modify
commands.description: Modify existing Terraform files via diff preview

commands.name: plan
commands.description: Trigger a Terraform plan via GitHub Actions

commands.name: apply
commands.description: Trigger a Terraform apply via GitHub Actions

commands.name: workspace
commands.description: List or select Terraform workspaces (GitHub Environments)

commands.name: bootstrap
commands.description: Bootstrap a new repository with Terraform workspace patterns

commands.name: varset
commands.description: Manage variable sets for an organization

commands.name: search
commands.description: Search GitHub org repos for Terraform code examples and documentation

languageModelTools.name: terraform_run_plan
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Triggers a terraform plan via GitHub Actions workflow_dispatch on the specified workspace (GitHub Environment). Returns the run URL and streams logs.

workspace.description: The GitHub Environment name (Terraform workspace) to plan. Defaults to current active workspace.

workingDirectory.description: The working directory within the repo containing the Terraform configuration.

languageModelTools.name: terraform_run_apply
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Triggers a terraform apply via GitHub Actions on the specified workspace. Requires confirmation. Returns the run URL.

workspace.description: The GitHub Environment name to apply.

languageModelTools.name: terraform_get_state
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Fetches and parses the Terraform state from the last successful run artifact. Returns a resource summary.

languageModelTools.name: terraform_list_workspaces
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Lists all GitHub Environments configured for the current repository, which correspond to Terraform workspaces.

languageModelTools.name: terraform_list_variables
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Lists all GitHub Actions variables and secrets (non-sensitive names only) for the current workspace or organization.

languageModelTools.name: terraform_set_variable
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Sets a GitHub Actions variable or secret in the specified scope. Sensitive values are stored as encrypted secrets.

sensitive.description: If true, stored as a GitHub Secret. If false, stored as a GitHub Variable.

category.description: Whether this is a TF_VAR_ or plain env var.

languageModelTools.name: terraform_generate_code
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Generates Terraform HCL from a natural language description, using detected provider versions and HappyPathway module patterns as context.

description.description: What infrastructure to create or modify.

targetFile.description: Target .tf file path. If omitted, creates a new file.

useModules.description: Prefer HappyPathway Terraform modules from the registry.

languageModelTools.name: terraform_bootstrap_workspace
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Sets up a GitHub repository with Terraform environments, GitHub Actions workflows, branch protection, and S3 backend config using HappyPathway patterns.

languageModelTools.name: terraform_read_config
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Returns the full contents of .vscode/terraform-workspace.json for the active workspace folder as JSON. Use this before making config changes so you know the current state.

languageModelTools.name: terraform_update_config
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Merges a partial patch into .vscode/terraform-workspace.json. Supports updating stateConfig (bucket, region, keyPrefix, dynamodbTable), compositeActions (action refs), compositeActionOrg, and repo metadata. Use terraform_read_config first to see current values.

stateConfig.description: Partial state backend config to merge in.

compositeActions.description: Partial composite action refs to merge in.

compositeActionOrg.description: GitHub org that owns the composite action repos.

languageModelTools.name: terraform_sync_workflows
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Generates GitHub Actions plan and apply workflow YAML files for every environment in the workspace config and writes them to .github/workflows/. Fetches live variable and secret names from GitHub to ensure every secret is explicitly referenced. Run this after terraform_update_config or whenever environments change.

languageModelTools.name: terraform_get_run_status
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Returns the most recent plan and apply workflow run statuses (status, conclusion, URL) for one or all configured workspaces. Use to check whether infra is in sync or a run is in progress.

workspace.description: Specific workspace (GitHub Environment) name. Omit to check all environments.

limit.description: Max runs to return per workflow. Default 3.

languageModelTools.name: terraform_search_tf_code
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Searches the current GitHub org's repositories for Terraform code matching a query. Returns file paths, repo names, and code snippets. Use to find examples, check how a resource is used across the org, or discover existing module usage.

languageModelTools.name: terraform_discover_workspace
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Auto-discovers reasonable defaults for the workspace config by scanning git remotes, .tf files (S3 backend, providers, regions, required_version), .tfvars files, .github/workflows YAML, and existing GitHub Environments. Returns a discovery summary AND a fully-populated suggested .vscode/terraform-workspace.json. Use this before asking the user for config values — most fields can be inferred. If `applyDefaults=true`, writes the config to disk; otherwise just returns it for review.

subdirectory.description: Optional subdirectory of the workspace folder to scan.

applyDefaults.description: If true, writes the suggested config to .vscode/terraform-workspace.json after discovery. Defaults to false.

languageModelTools.name: terraform_delete_variable
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Deletes a GitHub Actions variable or secret from the active repository or one of its environments. Set `sensitive=true` for secrets. For environment scope, supply `environment`. Requires user confirmation.

sensitive.description: True for secrets, false (default) for variables.

languageModelTools.name: terraform_resolve_variable
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Traces where a GitHub Actions variable is defined across org/repo/environment scopes for the active workspace and reports the effective (most specific) source. Use to debug 'why does this var have that value?' questions.

environment.description: Optional environment to include in the lookup.

languageModelTools.name: terraform_review_deployment
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Approves or rejects pending GitHub Actions deployment(s) gated by environment protection rules. Pass `runId` from a workflow run, `state` ('approved' or 'rejected'), an optional list of `environments` to filter to, and an optional `comment`. Requires user confirmation.

environments.description: Optional list of environment names to act on. Defaults to all approvable.

languageModelTools.name: terraform_lint_workflows
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Runs `actionlint` over the active folder's `.github/workflows/*.{yml,yaml}` and returns a structured list of issues (file, line, kind, message). Requires `actionlint` on PATH.

languageModelTools.name: terraform_check_drift
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Inspects the latest plan workflow run for each configured environment and returns environments whose plan exited with code 2 (pending changes / drift). Use to find environments that need an apply.

languageModelTools.name: terraform_scaffold_backend
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Generates Terraform code for an S3 state bucket + DynamoDB lock table (the bootstrap a team writes once per AWS account). Returns the HCL — does not apply it. Use to bootstrap remote state for a new account.

dynamodbTable.description: Name for the DynamoDB lock table.

kmsKeyAlias.description: Optional KMS key alias for SSE-KMS encryption (without 'alias/' prefix). Defaults to AES256.

languageModelTools.name: terraform_scaffold_oidc_trust
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Generates an AWS IAM trust policy JSON allowing GitHub Actions in a specific org/repo/environment to AssumeRoleWithWebIdentity via OIDC. Returns the JSON — does not apply it.

githubOrg.description: GitHub org/owner that will assume the role.

repo.description: Optional repo name; omit to allow any repo in the org.

environment.description: Optional environment name; omit to allow any environment.

oidcProvider.description: Override the default OIDC provider host (defaults to token.actions.githubusercontent.com).

languageModelTools.name: terraform_scaffold_from_template
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Creates a new GitHub repository from a template repository via POST /repos/{template_owner}/{template_repo}/generate. Use this for the 'start a new module / service from a template' workflow. Returns the new repo's URL and clone URL. Requires the 'repo' scope on the target owner.

templateOwner.description: Owner of the template repository (user or org).

templateRepo.description: Name of the template repository. Must be marked as a template on GitHub.

newRepoOwner.description: Owner for the new repo. Defaults to the authenticated user; supply an org name to create under an org.

description.description: Optional description for the new repository.

includeAllBranches.description: Include all branches from the template, not just the default branch. Default: false.

languageModelTools.name: terraform_scaffold_module_repo
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Generates the standard Terraform *module* repo skeleton (USAGE.md Pattern 1) into the active workspace folder: main.tf, variables.tf, outputs.tf, versions.tf, examples/<name>/, README.md with terraform-docs markers, .gitignore, and .terraform-docs.yml. Use this when starting a new reusable module from scratch in an empty repo. For creating a GitHub repo from an existing template, use terraform_scaffold_from_template instead. Existing files are skipped unless 'overwrite' is true.

moduleName.description: Module repo name, e.g. 'terraform-aws-vpc'. Drives README headings and the local module name in examples.

provider.description: Primary provider key, e.g. 'aws', 'azurerm', 'google', 'kubernetes'. Drives versions.tf required_providers.

description.description: Optional one-line description used in the README tagline.

exampleNames.description: Folder names under examples/ to scaffold. Defaults to ['basic'].

includeDevcontainer.description: Also write .devcontainer/devcontainer.json for VS Code Dev Containers. Default: false.

targetDirectory.description: Subdirectory of the active workspace folder to scaffold into. Defaults to the workspace root. Path traversal ('..') is rejected.

overwrite.description: If true, replace existing files. If false (default), existing files are reported as skipped.

terraformWorkspace.repoOrg.description: Default GitHub organization for new workspaces

terraformWorkspace.agent.enabled.description: Enable the autonomous agent. Polls GitHub Issues for the configured trigger label and attempts to resolve them via the registered Terraform tools.

terraformWorkspace.agent.owners.description: GitHub orgs/users whose issue queues the agent should scan.

terraformWorkspace.agent.triggerLabel.description: Issue label that flags an issue for the autonomous agent.

terraformWorkspace.agent.pollIntervalMin.description: How often the agent wakes itself up to scan the queue.

terraformWorkspace.agent.runOnFocus.description: Also wake the agent when VS Code regains focus.

terraformWorkspace.agent.maxTasksPerTick.description: Maximum tasks the agent will attempt in a single wake-up.

terraformWorkspace.agent.maxIterations.description: Hard ceiling on tool-call iterations per task to prevent runaway loops.

terraformWorkspace.agent.autonomyLevel.description: How autonomously the agent acts. `observe` only records findings; `draft-pr` opens PRs for review; `merge` merges when confident.

terraformWorkspace.agent.learner.enabled.description: Continuously ingest knowledge from repos topic-tagged with the configured learning topic. Runs at the start of every agent tick.

terraformWorkspace.agent.learner.owners.description: GitHub orgs/users to scan for learning-tagged repos.

terraformWorkspace.agent.learner.topic.description: GitHub repo topic that opts a repo into continuous knowledge ingestion.

terraformWorkspace.agent.learner.reposPe.description: Maximum repos the learner ingests per tick (across all owners).

terraformWorkspace.agent.learner.commits.description: Maximum recent commits inspected per repo per tick.

terraformWorkspace.compositeActionOrg.description: GitHub org containing composite action repos (aws-auth, gh-auth, terraform-init, etc.)

terraformWorkspace.defaultRunnerGroup.description: Default runner group label for GitHub Actions

terraformWorkspace.defaultStateRegion.description: Default AWS region for Terraform state S3 buckets

terraformWorkspace.preferOpenTofu.description: Prefer OpenTofu (tofu) over Terraform CLI in generated workflows

terraformWorkspace.useLocalActions.description: Scaffold composite actions into the repo at .github/actions/* and reference them locally instead of pulling from an external GitHub org. Recommended.

terraformWorkspace.aiModel.description: Language model family to use for code generation

terraformWorkspace.driftCheckMinutes.description: Minutes between automatic drift checks (0 disables the timer; the `Terraform: Check Drift` command still works on demand).

terraformWorkspace.enableTelemetry.description: Send anonymous usage telemetry. Off by default; respects VS Code's global telemetry setting.

walkthroughs.title: Get Started with Terraform Workspace
walkthroughs.description: Bootstrap a Terraform repo from a template, then drive plans/applies through GitHub Actions with AI in the loop.

steps.title: Scaffold a new repo from a template
steps.description:
Start here if you don't have a repo open yet. Generate a new GitHub repository from a template — no folder required.
[Scaffold From Template](command:terraform.scaffoldFromTemplate)

steps.title: Sign in & diagnose GitHub scopes
steps.description:
This extension talks to GitHub for everything. Confirm your token has `repo`, `read:org`, and `workflow`.
[Diagnose GitHub Auth Scopes](command:terraform.diagnoseAuth)

steps.title: Configure your workspace
steps.description:
Open the configuration panel to fill in repo, environments, and backend settings.
[Configure Workspace](command:terraform.configureWorkspace)

steps.title: Wire OIDC trust & remote backend
steps.description:
Generate the AWS IAM trust policy and the S3+DynamoDB backend HCL. No long-lived AWS keys.
[Scaffold OIDC Trust](command:terraform.scaffoldOidcTrust)
[Scaffold Backend](command:terraform.scaffoldBackend)

steps.title: Lint generated workflows
steps.description:
Run `actionlint` over generated workflows to catch syntax issues before pushing.
[Lint Workflows](command:terraform.lintWorkflows)

steps.title: Operate via the chat participant
steps.description:
Plans, variable resolution, drift checks, org-wide HCL search — all through `@terraform`.
[Open Chat](command:workbench.action.chat.open?%7B%22query%22%3A%22%40terraform+%22%7D)

steps.title: Check for drift
steps.description:
Manually trigger a drift check across environments.
[Check Drift](command:terraform.checkDrift)

scripts.clean: node -e "const fs=require('fs');for(const p of ['dist','coverage','.vitest-cache']){try{fs.rmSync(p,{recursive:true,force:true})}catch{}};for(const f of fs.readdirSync('.')){if(f.endsWith('.vsix'))fs.rmSync(f)}"
